# Graffito Short V9 Shots 12.30.24

## Scene 1- Elementary School-School lets out, meet Tony

**Shot 1:** Gentle pan down from the sky following a pigeon, then maybe others join as they land on ledges at the school. There are a lot of other pigeons sitting on ledges. GENTEL afternoon light. Pull back from the pigeons, resolving in a symmetrical tableau of the school.

Beat

**Same Shot (1)...** Doors burst open. Explosion of bodies is mostly seen in disassembled parts of people: heads, hands, tee shirts, pony tails, knapsacks, sneakers, food, phones. Can be some 3d and some animatic burst style collage of images. I'm open until we try it. My instinct is mix. This is Tony's sensory way of perceiving; he freezes and lets the sensory wave pass over him. the Initially it should feel overwhelming and unsettling. When the police attack is the first time Tony marshals this into an ability, which he soon masters and releases the **Can's** magic

**Same Shot (1)...** storm clears revealing Tony, head down in a notebook.

**Note:** Need to figure out hanging curls?

**Same Shot (1)...** pushes into a close up on Tony as he looks up.

**Note 2:** Still figuring out what eyes can do and what talking means and how faces express in our language. And how bodies express emotion. I don't think it's about articulation. I wonder about more of a south park array of expressions. But since there is a photo basis to the faces it might end up being more about the eye brows and lip flaps.

***

## Scene 2- Street Day. Bumping into the mean kids.

**Shot 2** starts as POV on (moving) notebook, while Tony's drawing with his feet walking below. We see the notebook carefully.

Moment of impact with other kids can be a simpler version of the opening cacophony. A quick flash of disorientation that reveals Tony on the ground, kids standing over in a wide shot.

**Sidebar-** Possibly, in these moments, he turns to his **Sketchbook** where he makes the terrifying 3D world into a cubistic set of 2D single frames. Crooked. Curious if there is a connection between the sketchbook and the animatic sensory burst. Might be too complicated. But Sketchbook does give us a window into Tony.

**Shot 3** or post animatic continuation of Shot 2- Tony's Phone being kicked as it flashes Monk's message. But Since we Hear Monk say: *I'm Back* not sure how much we have to read it. Maybe it's not so easy to read as phone is kicked away. Or maybe it's cool. See monks face spinning on the phone. Tony takes off running from this shot.

**Shot 4-** fast track with Tony running towards our retreating camera, widening angle of the kids staring at him in the BG. Meaning he gets bigger and they get smaller.

***

## Scene 3- Tony Running ON the Street Montage- test sequence

Sensory Montage of Tony running. A more ordered version of his internal cacophony. Starts abstractle, tactile; how Tony sees. After the near miss with the Gypsy cab morphs into a drone shot that teleports us into Grandmas. Establishes magically real fable-ness.

**Shot 5:** Feet pounding
**Shot 6:** Hand over Graffiti- graffiti mural should unfold with its own story as he runs by in CU
**Shot 7:** Forest of adult legs that he slaloms through
**Shot 8:** Tony's mouth yelling Monk. Eyes searching Side to Side- not in front
**Shot 9:** Bam! Animatic Cab near miss
**Shot 10:** Starts in ECU on Tony's terrified eyes. Pulls back as he mentally regroups and starts running again. Keeps pulling back and up becoming a drone shot of the entire hood.

***

## Scene 4- Grandmas Apartment- Monk and Tony dance and paint.

**Same Shot 10 (cont.)** Camera pulls from street into Grandma's Apt. Levels off, passes by a spinning Monk and pans 180degrees at the front door as Tony bursts in. Pans back another 180 degrees and settle on Tony and Monk Dancing to Fela.

**Shot 11:** 2 shot of Monk and Tonys dialog, we hear grandma before we see her, she enters bottom of frame and walks in so monk is on one side and Tony on the other, while the camera is over her silhouetted back.
**Shot 12:** Up angle on Grandma, holding the tray, looking stern
**Shot 13:** Down angle on Tony and Monk. Monk spinning chases Tony out of frame, follow Tony as he clutches his Stuffed **Elephant.**

**Note:** paint flies like confetti this entire time. The dance devolves into a few animatic frames, showing us the room, the paintings etc. The continuous kinetic energy that started when Tony started to run is broken by Grandma's line, which we hear before we see her.

***

## Scene 5- Grandma's Kitchen Convo

**Shot 14:** Medium 2 shot down the long way of a galley kitchen. They are jammed up against each other, spinning and turning. Working together to do the dishes.
**Shots 15/16:** CUs on Grandma and Tony to cover dialog.

***

## Scene 6- Sleeping Alcove

**Shot 17:** Wide pan around the quiet dark living room, night street seen through the window, find Monk sitting on the bed with Tony, push in.

***

## Scene 7-9- Graffito Story in B and W.

**Shot(s) 18:** Objective camera: News or security cam footage? Set: Use our same street in B and W. When we see Color it's only in the street art being painted and possibly Graffito themselves. AHA moment when Scribe paints the orange door. Later, Tony paints the same Orange Door.

**Note:** This is where we see Tony's first imagining of how the **Magic Can** functions. But since Tony is not the sprayer it should feel and look different than when he actually sprays it himself.
**Note 2:** The phenomena of the Can's Spray and its subsequent magic relates to Tony's image burst sensory reactions. Somehow, that liability in real life is an asset in the world of our story.
**Note 3- General Can note:** Rather than moments being blown apart into an image burst, maybe using the can emits an ethereal paint cloud, which as it dissipates, reveals images that align into 3D forms as they come back into focus? Or some other alchemical congealing.

**Note 4-Way out idea:** The Graffito story is all imagined as stylized Comic book pages. Panning across and through the cells. A series of paintings. Still working this out, but my instinct is that the Graffito Story should look other, possibly mythic and outside of Tony's visual language. Should there be a Graffito Comic book? Maybe in the feature...

***

## Scene 10- Back in bed, Graffito Story over.

**Shot 19:** Med 2 Shot. Starts in a 2 shot on bed like scene 6. Then goes under the covers and plays in the blanket tent. Light source shines through the blanket. They go to sleep in this shot.

***

## Scene 11- Monks gone

**Shot 20:** Time lapse leaves us in same shot but without Monk. C/U Tony's face He calls to Monk, but with no response whips the blanket off in same shot revealing the dark living room. Only light comes from the window. Tony looks to after not finding Monk. Cut off his look out.

***

## Scene 12- Spinning on the Corner

**Shot 21:** Tony enters tableau of Monk Spinning in a pyramid of street light. Once he enters the shot spins with them. Eventually, they stop and the BG continues to spin around them. Tony runs out of this shot and we stay on Monk with the spinning BG.

***

## Scene 13- The Mural and the Cops

**Shot 22:** The same tilt down wide of the school that opens the film. Monk and Tony are painting a wall in the front. Camera pushes in, then starts a journey around the celestial sky Monk is painting. Van Gogh meets Basquiat meets the Bronx in 1977. Its energetic but it's also lyrical and fluid. As we journey around the sky Monk dances in and out of frame as he throws paint around. Relates to him painting in scene 4. Until the Cops show up which ends fluid and lyrical. It's now kinetic and disjointed. Scarry.
**Shot 23:** Tony's POV of the Magic Can. He follows it wherever it goes.
**Shot 24:** Hand Held- in and out of Tony's POV. Cops arrive in this shot.
**Shot 25:** Cop Body Cam. Police are all sound and light. And POV. More intense when close.
**Shot 26:** Hard pans from Monk to Tony, once Monk is caught. No matter, they stay connected.
**Shot 27:** Tony's POV as The Magic Can Sprays. Could also be Shot 22. Ends with him visually connecting to Monk through the Orange Door as it closes.

**Note 1-** This is the moment we've all been waiting for. It's the resolution of the animatic cacophonous sensory style, which now is an act of creation. Elements of chaos start to register. It also connects with the flowing and spinning of Monk. And with Music. These are our ingredients. Need to convey that it's the inverse of Tonys liabilities that give him his power.

**Note 2-** The process of Can creation is more detailed this first time, but later as he's creating it can look like the Can's mist of cacophony continues to revel/ create more and more.

**Note 3-** Sometimes, he makes the place as he steps on. But later, once the realm is created then he is just in the place, and creates what he needs in the moment.

***

## Scene 14- Space

**Shot 28:** Wide Gravity Shot. We pass Sandra Bullock. JK. IN a super wide angle, we show space being created into the void, like the big bang.
**Shot 29:** MCU on Tony. He's figuring it out. Realizing his power.

**Note:** As Tony steps onto the Street, it becomes day. The street is similar to the opening running street in Tony's hood. Maybe we paint the buildings a color? Monk's Mural is the sky, there are distant mountains, where apartment buildings use to be on the horizon. The pedestrians are all animals. No humans

***

## Scene 15- Emerging from the woods

**Shot 30:** Tonys POV as he walks and sprays out the Street. Nozzle of the Can in the foreground. Naked Gun Siren shot. Once Tony's Magic Spray reaches the horizon, we first hear, then see ML emerge. She needs a smoke trail so we can spot her. In same shot she gets closer and bigger until she fills the frame bearing down on Tony's POV.
**Shot 31/32:** Singles to cover the dialog. Need to intercut with Tony's POV.
**Shot 33:** A two shot to describe the action of ML taking the Can from Tony. This may also be possible in the POV. She slams into the building in his POV. He sees the crumbs in the POV.
**Shot 34:** 2 Shot. Elephant sidles up. Push into Elephant's Cu.

***

## Scene 16- Castle Wall

**Shot 35:** C/U as Elephants trunk inhales crumb. Pulls back into two-shot as he pops it in his mouth, reveal Tony on his shoulders, over his head. pull back more into a Wide shot of them emerging from the woods at dawn.
**Shot 36:** High angle 2 Shot of Elephant and Tony at the bottom of the wall.
**Shot 37:** Low angle single on ML.
**Shot 38:** Single on Tony as he levitates with Elephants help. Pulls out to reveal Elephant.

**Note:** Castle should relate to School?

## Scene 17- Castle Wall- Morning

**Shot 39:** High Angle, showing danger of Tony's situation on the impossibly tall wall. Might resolve into a 2 shot with ML as Tony sits down.
**Shot 40:** 2 Shot on ML and Tony- widens to a high angle 3 shot with Elephant below.
**Shots 41/42:** Singles on Tony and ML.
**Shot 43:** Spinning 2 shot similar to spinning at the street lamp with Monk.
**Shot 44:** CU Elephant calling to Tony as he falls
**Shot 45:** Tony's POV as he plumets fast. Need Dynamic energetic scary angles
**Shot 46:** CU. Tony is determined, not scared any more.
**Shot 47:** Dragon flying straight down trying to save Tony.

**Shot 47:** Wide of the School Mural bathed in cop light. Door opens. Push in to Tony in the Door
**Shot 48:** MCU Monk Reacts.
**Shot 49:** 2 shot on Monk and Tony as the door closes. Pulls back out to wide on mural as painted star shoots by.
